Title: Sucrose synthases are not involved in starch synthesis in Arabidopsis leaves

Common_Genes.Gene_Name: SUS1; SUS2; SUS3; SUS4; SUS5; SUS6
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: transitory carbon and energy reserve that buffers carbohydrate supply during fluctuating light and supports survival at night
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of SUS activity does not reduce ADP-glucose or transitory starch accumulation in leaves; SUS is not required for leaf starch synthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis sus1234 mutants lacking the four SUS isoforms expressed in mesophyll cells had wild-type ADP-glucose and starch levels in leaves. 2. CRISPR/Cas9-generated sextuple sus123456 mutants lacked detectable soluble SUS activity yet retained wild-type diel patterns of ADP-glucose, Glc6P, Glc1P, soluble sugars, and starch. 3. Therefore, sucrose synthases do not make a significant contribution to ADP-glucose supply for transitory starch synthesis in Arabidopsis leaves under the tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: quadruple mutant analysis; CRISPR/Cas9 sextuple knockout generation; enzyme activity assay for SUS; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; diel starch measurement; 13CO2 pulse labeling comparison of ADPG and UDPG kinetics
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: long-day growth (16 h photoperiod) under standard laboratory conditions; illuminated leaf harvest with strict avoidance of shading; 13CO2 pulse labeling under ambient CO2
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: SUS1-SUS4 are the isoforms expressed in mesophyll cells; all six SUS genes are expressed in siliques and/or seeds
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: SUS1-SUS4 expressed in mesophyll cells; all six SUS genes expressed in siliques and/or seeds
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: sucrose synthase family; glycosyltransferase-domain enzymes involved in sucrose cleavage/synthesis
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: soluble/cytosolic activity assayed in rosettes and siliques
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: metabolite profiling by LC-MS/MS; 13CO2 isotope-labeling metabolite analysis; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a useful target for increasing transitory starch synthesis in Arabidopsis leaves under standard conditions; removing SUS genes causes only mild growth phenotypes and does not impair leaf starch accumulation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: sextuple mutants showed only mild growth phenotypes under standard laboratory conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This study directly rejects the controversial model proposing cytosolic SUS-derived ADP-glucose import into chloroplasts for leaf starch synthesis in Arabidopsis. SUS remains background-relevant for sucrose metabolism but is not causal for the target metabolite in this leaf system.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: transitory carbon and energy reserve that supports nighttime metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: chloroplastic consensus pathway for ADP-glucose production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: interconverts glucose 6-phosphate and glucose 1-phosphate in plastids to supply substrate for ADP-glucose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate precursor pool for chloroplastic ADP-glucose synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key required step in the consensus chloroplastic pathway; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of plastidial PGM causes very low ADP-glucose and near absence of leaf starch, supporting the chloroplastic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In pgm mutants, ADP-glucose levels in illuminated leaves were far below wild type. 2. pgm mutants accumulated less than 1% of wild-type starch by ZT12. 3. These results support plastidial PGM as an essential enzyme in the consensus pathway of leaf starch synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (pgm); LC-MS/MS quantification of ADPG, Glc6P, Glc1P; starch measurement across diel cycle
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: illumination and diel time course; light-harvest conditions avoiding shading artifacts
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Confirms plastidial carbon flux through PGM is indispensable for transitory starch synthesis; disruption strongly compromises starch accumulation, so this step is critical to preserve in engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here mainly as a decisive positive-control pathway gene validating the chloroplastic route for ADPG formation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: transitory carbon and energy reserve that supports nighttime metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: chloroplastic consensus pathway for ADP-glucose production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: synthesizes ADP-glucose from glucose 1-phosphate and ATP in chloroplasts
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms the substrate used for starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: established as essential and central for chloroplastic ADP-glucose production; treated as key control step in this study
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of AGPase function causes very low ADP-glucose and almost starchless leaves, demonstrating that chloroplastic AGPase is the primary source of ADP-glucose for leaf starch synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. adg1 mutants had ADP-glucose levels about 20-fold lower than wild type in illuminated leaves. 2. adg1 mutants accumulated less than 1% of wild-type starch by ZT12. 3. These data support the conclusion that ADP-glucose required for leaf starch synthesis is synthesized primarily by chloroplastic AGPase.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis (adg1); LC-MS/MS ADPG measurement with optimized no-shade sampling; diel starch measurement
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: illumination and diel time course; light harvest avoiding shading; brief shading further depressed already low ADPG
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strongly supports chloroplastic AGPase as the critical enzymatic node to target when modifying transitory starch synthesis in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper’s main mechanistic conclusion is that chloroplastic AGPase, not SUS, supplies leaf ADP-glucose.
